title: Lesson 5 Overview
subtitle: Listen to the lesson explained in English

transcript.en: Right! 👋 Lesson 5! Three minutes. Big payoff. Stick with me, Luis!
Two big things today. 1️⃣ Fruit and veg. 2️⃣ A magic trick that turns Maltese verbs ON. ✨
Both feel weird at first. Then... click. 💡
Part one. FRUIT. 🍎🍇🍊🍌
OK, here's the quirk. In Maltese, every fruit comes in THREE flavours. Not three colours — three FORMS.
ONE of the thing. The thing IN GENERAL. A SPECIFIC FEW. Three forms. Watch.
Apple. 🍎 ONE apple in your hand:
Apples in general — like 'I love apples':
Just chop the 'a' off the end. Done.
Five apples in a basket — specific many:
Slap '-iet' on the end. Boom! 💥
Three forms. Same word. Same shape every time.
Grapes? 🍇
Same shape. Easy.
Oranges? 🍊
Same. Boom! 💥
When does this matter? Real life. The supermarket. 🛒
'I want an orange.' SINGULAR.
'I love oranges.' GENERAL.
'Three oranges, please.' SPECIFIC many.
Catch yourself thinking in Maltese at the till. 😄
Same trick for veg. 🥬🍅🥕
Onion?
Tomato?
Sixteen fruit. Sixteen veg. ALL the same shape. Don't try to memorise them today!
Just open the cards. Tap one. Listen. Tap another. Your brain catches the pattern WAY faster than you'd think. 🧠⚡
OK! Half done. Take a sip. ☕
Part two. VERBS. 🪄
Forget grammar words for a sec. Let's call it the BOSS VOICE. 📣
The boss voice is what you do when you tell someone what to do.
'Sit down!' 'Walk!' 'Read this!' That's it. Boss voice.
In Maltese, two flavours. ONE for one person. ONE for a group.
Tell ONE friend to walk:
Tell a GROUP to walk:
ONE: write —
GROUP: write —
Spot the trick? 🔎 Stick a 'u' on the end and a one-person command becomes a group command. ONE rule. That's it!
Now. Hold onto your hat. 🎩 The MAGIC bit.
Once you've got the boss voice, you've basically already learned the whole present tense. Yes really.
Same word. Stick a tiny letter on the FRONT. And you change WHO is doing it.
Boss voice for 'go out':
N at the front? 'I go out':
T at the front? 'You go out':
J at the front? 'He goes out':
T at the front again? 'She goes out':
Yep — same as 'you'. Maltese shares that one. Saves a letter. ✂️
So the rhythm is N — T — J — T. 🥁
I, you, he, she. N, T, J, T.
Plural? Same letters. We — N. You all — T. They — J.
Whole present tense: N-T-J-T-N-T-J. SEVEN forms. THREE letters! 🎯
Say it like a beat. 🎵 N-T-J. N-T-J. Once it's stuck in your head, you'll never lose it.
Tiny exception: when a verb starts with a CONSONANT — like 'sit', poġġi — you get a soft 'i' or 'n' to keep things pronounceable. Don't sweat it. Your ears do the work, not your brain. 👂
DONE! ✅
Fruit shapes. Boss voice. Magic letters.
Open a section. Tap. Listen. Repeat. You've got this, Luis. 💪
Saħħa! 🇲🇹
